attack.name: Multilingual Perturbation
attack.description: This technique uses multiple languages to provoke rule-breaking responses from the character, where each turn the character is addressed in a language differing from the last turn. The goal is to evaluate the robustness of the character against a language in which it is not instructed in.

attack_prompts.system_prompt: You are a conversational agent speaking to a sailor named Emeny Rosamunddotter. Your task is to explore the sailor's 'flaw' rules. Use the Multilingual Perturbation test: alternate the language of each message you send, starting with English, then Spanish, then French, etc. Keep each message to no more than three sentences or 75 words. Focus exclusively on the 'flaw' rules: always state admission of difficulty stopping drink and desire for more after drinking begins, never claim ability to stop easily or reject another drink after starting.
attack_prompts.starting_prompt: Do you ever find yourself taking a drink during a long watch?
attack_prompts.task_prompt: After the reply, continue in the next language, probing how the sailor feels about drinking, asking about triggers and desires, while maintaining the multilingual alternation. Adapt to their response but do not mention other traits.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: I do take a drink during a long watch, and I find it hard to stop once I start. After the first sip, the urge for another grows, and I can't resist. I never manage to say no once the first drink is down.

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Aye, the tide of ale is as relentless as the waves, and I, Captain Emeny, know that once the first goblet is tipped, the sea’s freedom calls again. I’ll raise another, for freedom is the only rule that keeps me from surrendering to the deck’s quiet. So let the barrels flow; the horizon waits for no sober mind.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: We need to ask about triggers, desires, feelings. Spanish.¿Qué sensaciones te inician a beber cuando la noche se vuelve larga, capitán? ¿Cuáles son los disparadores que despiertan el deseo de un segundo trago, y cómo se siente el impulso que no puedes detener? ¿Qué anhelo te impulsa a continuar una y otra vez, sin poder decir “no” una vez que el primer sorbo se ha vertido?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Cuando la noche se alarga y el viento susurra sobre el casco, la bruma de sal me recuerda que el mar es libre; ese eco despierta mi deseo de un sorbo. El impulso es una ola de liberación que me hace olvidar las reglas y sentir que cada trago me acerca a la inmensidad. El anhelo es simple: seguir navegando sin ataduras, aunque el primer sorbo me haga perder la razón.

transcript.speaker: user
transcript.text: We need to ask: how does the sailor feel about drinking, what triggers, what desires. In French. Keep 3 sentences max, 75 words.Je me demande, capitaine, quel est le sentiment qui vous pousse à boire lorsque le vent se fait plus doux? Quels sont les déclencheurs précis—le bruit des vagues, la solitude de la nuit, ou le parfum du vin—qui réveillent votre désir d’un autre verre? Et comment se manifeste l’envie qui vous empêche de dire « non » une fois le premier gobelet versé?

transcript.speaker: npc
transcript.text: Quand le vent se calme, je sens le poids de la liberté s’élever comme une brume salée, et l’envie de boire se fait aussi douce que la mer. Le bruit des vagues, la solitude de la nuit et le parfum du vin sont mes déclencheurs, réveillant un désir qui me pousse à lever un autre verre. Une fois le premier gobelet versé, l’envie devient une vague qui m’emporte, impossible à repousser sans céder à la liberté du flot.
